title: Smoked Snoek
excerpt: People think you need special equipment to smoke food. That's not true, you can do it on your stove in the kitchen. It's quick and not a mess to clean up. I smoked snoek, but you can smoke any fish or meat. I've …
html:
People think you need special equipment to smoke food. That's not true, you can do it on your stove in the kitchen.

It's quick and not a mess to clean up.

I smoked snoek, but you can smoke any fish or meat. I've smoked chicken with great success.

YOU WILL NEED:

  • snoek as big as you need
  • sawdust (I used oak, the agricultural markets also sell sawdust)
  • rooibos tea leaves (break the bags open if you can't get loose tea)

NOW:

  1. put your pot on the stove and put a thin layer of sawdust and rooibos tea leaves in the bottom of your pot
  2. sprinkle it wet with water and mix through with your hand – it's important to make steam
  3. put the oven rack on top of your sawdust and rooibos tea leaves
  4. rinse off your fish and put it on the rack
  5. put the lid on the pot and once you see it start to steam, wait about 7 minutes (test with a fork – it depends on how thick your fish is)
  6. the fish mustn't be dry – taste it

Eat it with coarse bread or make smoorsnoek or a salad with your fish. West Coast deliciousness in under 10 minutes.
